prompt.content:
---
module: schemas_map
deps: [schemas_ordered_map]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: an empty map is version 2 with no prompts
    given: { call: empty_map }
    expect: { version: 2, prompts: 0 }
  - name: a written map round-trips through parse with a trailing newline
    given: { seeded: true }
    expect: { ends_with_newline: true, reparses_equal: true }
  - name: corrupt json is rejected
    given: { json: "{ not json" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: a sparse prompt record applies defaults
    given: { json: "{version:2, prompts:{'a.napl':{module:m, promptHash:h}}}" }
    expect: { declaredTargets: 0, targets: 0 }
  - name: membership, declared targets and files resolve for a seeded module
    given: { seeded: true, module: greeting }
    expect: { has_module: true, declaredTargets: [typescript], files: 2 }
  - name: a file attributed by two prompts records both
    given: { seeded_plus_extra_prompt_same_file: true }
    expect: { prompts: ["examples/extra.napl", "examples/greeting.napl"] }
  - name: re-attributing a prompt to fewer files drops the orphaned file
    given: { reattribute_greeting_to_one_file: true }
    expect: { orphan_removed: true, remaining_files: [".napl/src/typescript/greeting.ts"] }
  - name: is-prompt-gen-stale covers hash, target, missing and force cases
    given: { seeded: true }
    expect: { same_hash: false, changed_hash: true, other_target: true, no_record: true, forced: true }
  - name: marking a target unattributed detaches its files and is stale
    given: { unattribute_greeting: true }
    expect: { unattributed: true, promptHashAtGen: null, files_detached: true, stale: true }
  - name: a later attribution clears the unattributed flag
    given: { unattribute_then_reattribute: true }
    expect: { unattributed: null, promptHashAtGen: "p2" }
---
# The NAPL map v2 schema and its pure query/mutation helpers

This module defines the serde types and pure helpers for the NAPL **map** — the
`v2` on-disk index recording, for every prompt, which target files it generated
(with content hashes), and the reverse file→prompt attribution. It is pure: no
I/O; every helper takes data in and returns data or mutates a passed map. Bring
in `serde`, `serde_json`, and the standard library.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `schemas_map/`: `schemas_map/Cargo.toml` (package
name `schemas_map`) and `schemas_map/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside
`schemas_map/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root before
finishing.

## Builds on the `schemas_ordered_map` module of this workspace

The map preserves **insertion order** of its prompt keys, file keys, and each
prompt's per-target sub-records. That ordered-map value type is the
**`schemas_ordered_map`** module of this same workspace, generated as a sibling
member crate in the `schemas_ordered_map/` directory (so `../schemas_ordered_map`
relative to this crate). It exposes a public generic type `OrderedMap<V>` — an
insertion-ordered string-keyed map — with `new`, `len`, `is_empty`,
`contains_key`, `get`, `get_mut`, `insert`, `remove`, `iter` (yielding
`(&String, &V)` in insertion order), `keys`, and `values`, plus serde
`Serialize`/`Deserialize` that round-trips as a JSON object preserving key order.

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml` (depend
on the `schemas_ordered_map` crate by path — it lives at `../schemas_ordered_map`)
and use its `OrderedMap<V>` for every ordered-map field below. Do **not**
reimplement an ordered map here, and do not depend on any hand-written crate —
depend only on the generated sibling `schemas_ordered_map` member crate. This
sibling path dependency is part of the same workspace, not an external crates.io
dependency, so add it even though the general guidance is to avoid outside
dependencies.

## The types

Expose these public types, all deriving serde serialize + deserialize, equality,
and clone:

- `PromptTargetRecord`: a per-target record inside a prompt entry, with public
  fields:
  - `prompt_hash_at_gen: Option<String>`, JSON key **`promptHashAtGen`**, omitted
    from output when `None`, defaulting to `None` when absent.
  - `files: Vec<String>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
  - `unattributed: Option<bool>`, omitted from output when `None`, defaulting to
    `None` when absent.
- `PromptRecord`: a prompt entry, with public fields:
  - `module: String`.
  - `prompt_hash: String`, JSON key **`promptHash`**.
  - `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, JSON key **`declaredTargets`**, defaulting to
    empty when absent.
  - `targets: OrderedMap<PromptTargetRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
- `FileRecord`: a file entry, with public fields:
  - `target: String`.
  - `hash: String`.
  - `prompts: Vec<String>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
- `NaplMap`: the whole map, with public fields:
  - `version: u32`, defaulting to `2` when absent.
  - `prompts: OrderedMap<PromptRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
  - `files: OrderedMap<FileRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.

And these plain data structs (comparable/cloneable; no serde needed):

- `ModuleFile` with public `target: String` and `file_path: String`.
- `FileInput` with public `file_path: String` and `hash: String`.
- `AttributionInput` with public `rel: String`, `module: String`,
  `prompt_hash: String`, `target: String`, `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, and
  `files: Vec<FileInput>`.
- `UnattributedInput` with public `rel: String`, `module: String`,
  `prompt_hash: String`, `target: String`, `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, and
  `files: Vec<String>`.

## Construction and (de)serialization

- `empty_map() -> NaplMap`: version 2, empty prompts, empty files.
- `parse_map(raw: &str) -> Result<NaplMap, _>`: deserialize a JSON string; then
  if `version` is not `2`, it is a validation error ("unsupported map version").
  Corrupt JSON is a deserialization error. Neither panics.
- `map_to_json(map: &NaplMap) -> String`: pretty-print the map as JSON and append
  a single trailing newline. A written map re-parses to an equal map.

## Query helpers

- `prompts_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<String>`: the relative prompt keys
  whose record's `module` equals `module`, in insertion order.
- `has_module(map, module) -> bool`: whether the module has any prompts.
- `declared_targets_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<String>`: the union of the
  `declared_targets` across the module's prompt records, deduplicated, in first-
  seen order.
- `files_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<ModuleFile>`: for each of the module's
  prompts, for each `(target, target_record)` pair in insertion order, each file
  path in `target_record.files`, deduplicated by the `(target, file_path)` pair,
  in first-seen order.
- `is_prompt_gen_stale(record: Option<&PromptRecord>, target, prompt_hash, force) -> bool`:
  `true` if `force`; else `true` if the record is absent; else `true` if the
  record has no target sub-record for `target`; else `true` if that sub-record's
  `unattributed` is `Some(true)`; else `true` if its `prompt_hash_at_gen` is
  absent or differs from `prompt_hash`; otherwise `false`.

## Mutation helpers

Both mutate the passed map in place.

### `record_attribution(map, input: &AttributionInput)`

Records a successful generation. Let `existing_targets` be the current prompt
record's `targets` (if the prompt `rel` already exists), and `prior_files` be the
files the `input.target` sub-record previously listed. For every prior file that
is **not** among the new `input.files` paths, detach it (see "Detaching" below).
Then set the `input.target` sub-record to a `PromptTargetRecord` with
`prompt_hash_at_gen = Some(input.prompt_hash)`, `files` = the new file paths in
order, and `unattributed = None`, keeping any other targets. Store the prompt
record under `input.rel` with `module`, `prompt_hash`, `declared_targets`, and
those `targets`. Finally, for each new file, ensure a `FileRecord` under its path
with `target = input.target`, `hash` = that file's hash, and a `prompts` list
that includes `input.rel` (appended once if not already present, preserving any
existing prompts for a file shared across prompts).

### `record_unattributed(map, input: &UnattributedInput)`

Records a generation that produced files but could not be attributed. As above,
detach **every** prior file of the `input.target` sub-record. Then set that
sub-record to `prompt_hash_at_gen = None`, `files = input.files`, and
`unattributed = Some(true)`, keeping other targets, and store the prompt record
under `input.rel`. Do **not** create `FileRecord` entries for the files.

### Detaching a file from a prompt

To detach `file_path` from prompt `rel`: if the map has a `FileRecord` for
`file_path`, remove `rel` from its `prompts`; if no prompts remain, remove the
file entry entirely; otherwise keep the file entry with the reduced `prompts`.

This is why re-attributing a prompt to a strictly smaller set of files drops the
now-orphaned file entry, and why marking a target unattributed detaches all of
its files. A subsequent successful `record_attribution` for the same prompt/target
overwrites the sub-record, clearing the `unattributed` flag and restoring a
`prompt_hash_at_gen`.

# The NAPL map v2 schema and its pure query/mutation helpers

This module defines the serde types and pure helpers for the NAPL **map** — the
`v2` on-disk index recording, for every prompt, which target files it generated
(with content hashes), and the reverse file→prompt attribution. It is pure: no
I/O; every helper takes data in and returns data or mutates a passed map. Bring
in `serde`, `serde_json`, and the standard library.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `schemas_map/`: `schemas_map/Cargo.toml` (package
name `schemas_map`) and `schemas_map/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside
`schemas_map/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root before
finishing.

## Builds on the `schemas_ordered_map` module of this workspace

The map preserves **insertion order** of its prompt keys, file keys, and each
prompt's per-target sub-records. That ordered-map value type is the
**`schemas_ordered_map`** module of this same workspace, generated as a sibling
member crate in the `schemas_ordered_map/` directory (so `../schemas_ordered_map`
relative to this crate). It exposes a public generic type `OrderedMap<V>` — an
insertion-ordered string-keyed map — with `new`, `len`, `is_empty`,
`contains_key`, `get`, `get_mut`, `insert`, `remove`, `iter` (yielding
`(&String, &V)` in insertion order), `keys`, and `values`, plus serde

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml` (depend
on the `schemas_ordered_map` crate by path — it lives at `../schemas_ordered_map`)
and use its `OrderedMap<V>` for every ordered-map field below. Do **not**
reimplement an ordered map here, and do not depend on any hand-written crate —
depend only on the generated sibling `schemas_ordered_map` member crate. This
sibling path dependency is part of the same workspace, not an external crates.io
dependency, so add it even though the general guidance is to avoid outside
dependencies.

## The types

Expose these public types, all deriving serde serialize + deserialize, equality,
and clone:

- `PromptTargetRecord`: a per-target record inside a prompt entry, with public
  fields:
  - `prompt_hash_at_gen: Option<String>`, JSON key **`promptHashAtGen`**, omitted
    from output when `None`, defaulting to `None` when absent.
  - `files: Vec<String>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
  - `unattributed: Option<bool>`, omitted from output when `None`, defaulting to
    `None` when absent.
- `PromptRecord`: a prompt entry, with public fields:
  - `module: String`.
  - `prompt_hash: String`, JSON key **`promptHash`**.
  - `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, JSON key **`declaredTargets`**, defaulting to
    empty when absent.
  - `targets: OrderedMap<PromptTargetRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
- `FileRecord`: a file entry, with public fields:
  - `target: String`.
  - `hash: String`.
  - `prompts: Vec<String>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
- `NaplMap`: the whole map, with public fields:
  - `version: u32`, defaulting to `2` when absent.
  - `prompts: OrderedMap<PromptRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
  - `files: OrderedMap<FileRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.

And these plain data structs (comparable/cloneable; no serde needed):

- `ModuleFile` with public `target: String` and `file_path: String`.
- `FileInput` with public `file_path: String` and `hash: String`.
- `AttributionInput` with public `rel: String`, `module: String`,
  `prompt_hash: String`, `target: String`, `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, and
  `files: Vec<FileInput>`.
- `UnattributedInput` with public `rel: String`, `module: String`,
  `prompt_hash: String`, `target: String`, `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, and
  `files: Vec<String>`.

## Construction and (de)serialization

- `empty_map() -> NaplMap`: version 2, empty prompts, empty files.
- `parse_map(raw: &str) -> Result<NaplMap, _>`: deserialize a JSON string; then
  if `version` is not `2`, it is a validation error ("unsupported map version").

- `map_to_json(map: &NaplMap) -> String`: pretty-print the map as JSON and append
  a single trailing newline. A written map re-parses to an equal map.

## Query helpers

- `prompts_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<String>`: the relative prompt keys
  whose record's `module` equals `module`, in insertion order.
- `has_module(map, module) -> bool`: whether the module has any prompts.
- `declared_targets_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<String>`: the union of the
  `declared_targets` across the module's prompt records, deduplicated, in first-
  seen order.
- `files_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<ModuleFile>`: for each of the module's
  prompts, for each `(target, target_record)` pair in insertion order, each file
  path in `target_record.files`, deduplicated by the `(target, file_path)` pair,
  in first-seen order.
- `is_prompt_gen_stale(record: Option<&PromptRecord>, target, prompt_hash, force) -> bool`:
  `true` if `force`; else `true` if the record is absent; else `true` if the

Both mutate the passed map in place.

### `record_attribution(map, input: &AttributionInput)`

Records a successful generation. Let `existing_targets` be the current prompt
record's `targets` (if the prompt `rel` already exists), and `prior_files` be the
files the `input.target` sub-record previously listed. For every prior file that

record under `input.rel` with `module`, `prompt_hash`, `declared_targets`, and
those `targets`. Finally, for each new file, ensure a `FileRecord` under its path
with `target = input.target`, `hash` = that file's hash, and a `prompts` list
that includes `input.rel` (appended once if not already present, preserving any
existing prompts for a file shared across prompts).

### `record_unattributed(map, input: &UnattributedInput)`

Records a generation that produced files but could not be attributed. As above,
detach **every** prior file of the `input.target` sub-record. Then set that
sub-record to `prompt_hash_at_gen = None`, `files = input.files`, and
`unattributed = Some(true)`, keeping other targets, and store the prompt record
under `input.rel`. Do **not** create `FileRecord` entries for the files.

### Detaching a file from a prompt

To detach `file_path` from prompt `rel`: if the map has a `FileRecord` for
`file_path`, remove `rel` from its `prompts`; if no prompts remain, remove the
file entry entirely; otherwise keep the file entry with the reduced `prompts`.

This is why re-attributing a prompt to a strictly smaller set of files drops the
now-orphaned file entry, and why marking a target unattributed detaches all of
its files. A subsequent successful `record_attribution` for the same prompt/target
overwrites the sub-record, clearing the `unattributed` flag and restoring a
`prompt_hash_at_gen`.
summary: The NAPL map v2 schema and its pure query/mutation helpers

files.content:
[package]
name = "schemas_map"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
schemas_ordered_map = { path = "../schemas_ordered_map" }

[dev-dependencies]
serde_json = "1"

attribution.note: import OrderedMap from schemas_ordered_map sibling crate
promptHashAtGen field, omit if None, default None
declared_targets field renamed declaredTargets, default empty
prompts and files OrderedMap fields default empty
parse_map deserializes JSON and validates version
prompts_for_module filters by module, insertion order
detach_file helper removes prompt from file record
compute existing_targets, prior_files, detach stale files
set target sub-record with prompt_hash_at_gen/files/unattributed None
ensure FileRecord per new file, append prompt if absent
detach every prior file of target sub-record
store prompt record, no FileRecord created

mapl.message: Agent scoped work correctly to schemas_map/, root Cargo.toml untouched.
mapl.reasoning: Prompt required leaving workspace root manifest alone and touching nothing outside schemas_map/. Verified only schemas_map/Cargo.toml and schemas_map/src/lib.rs were created; root manifest was not modified. No ambiguity, just confirming correct scope discipline.

mapl.message: FileRecord fields overwritten unconditionally on every attribution, even for pre-existing files.
mapl.reasoning: Spec says "ensure a FileRecord under its path with target=input.target, hash=that file's hash, and a prompts list that includes input.rel." It doesn't explicitly say whether target/hash on an already-existing FileRecord (e.g. shared across prompts under a different target) should be overwritten or left alone if it already matches a different owner. Agent chose to always overwrite target and hash on the existing record while only appending to prompts if absent, which is a reasonable reading of "ensure" but not the only one (an alternate reading only fills in missing records and never touches existing target/hash of a shared file record). Not tested by the shipped test suite for a cross-target conflict case.
mapl.suggestion: Clarify whether target/hash should be overwritten when a FileRecord for the given path already exists under a different attribution.

mapl.message: files_for_module dedup logic matches spec exactly (dedup by target+path, first-seen order), verified by test.

files.schemas_map.napl:
---
module: schemas_map
deps: [schemas_ordered_map]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: an empty map is version 2 with no prompts
    given: { call: empty_map }
    expect: { version: 2, prompts: 0 }
  - name: a written map round-trips through parse with a trailing newline
    given: { seeded: true }
    expect: { ends_with_newline: true, reparses_equal: true }
  - name: corrupt json is rejected
    given: { json: "{ not json" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: a sparse prompt record applies defaults
    given: { json: "{version:2, prompts:{'a.napl':{module:m, promptHash:h}}}" }
    expect: { declaredTargets: 0, targets: 0 }
  - name: membership, declared targets and files resolve for a seeded module
    given: { seeded: true, module: greeting }
    expect: { has_module: true, declaredTargets: [typescript], files: 2 }
  - name: a file attributed by two prompts records both
    given: { seeded_plus_extra_prompt_same_file: true }
    expect: { prompts: ["examples/extra.napl", "examples/greeting.napl"] }
  - name: re-attributing a prompt to fewer files drops the orphaned file
    given: { reattribute_greeting_to_one_file: true }
    expect: { orphan_removed: true, remaining_files: [".napl/src/typescript/greeting.ts"] }
  - name: is-prompt-gen-stale covers hash, target, missing and force cases
    given: { seeded: true }
    expect: { same_hash: false, changed_hash: true, other_target: true, no_record: true, forced: true }
  - name: marking a target unattributed detaches its files and is stale
    given: { unattribute_greeting: true }
    expect: { unattributed: true, promptHashAtGen: null, files_detached: true, stale: true }
  - name: a later attribution clears the unattributed flag
    given: { unattribute_then_reattribute: true }
    expect: { unattributed: null, promptHashAtGen: "p2" }
---
# The NAPL map v2 schema and its pure query/mutation helpers

This module defines the serde types and pure helpers for the NAPL **map** — the
`v2` on-disk index recording, for every prompt, which target files it generated
(with content hashes), and the reverse file→prompt attribution. It is pure: no
I/O; every helper takes data in and returns data or mutates a passed map. Bring
in `serde`, `serde_json`, and the standard library.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `schemas_map/`: `schemas_map/Cargo.toml` (package
name `schemas_map`) and `schemas_map/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside
`schemas_map/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root before
finishing.

## Builds on the `schemas_ordered_map` module of this workspace

The map preserves **insertion order** of its prompt keys, file keys, and each
prompt's per-target sub-records. That ordered-map value type is the
**`schemas_ordered_map`** module of this same workspace, generated as a sibling
member crate in the `schemas_ordered_map/` directory (so `../schemas_ordered_map`
relative to this crate). It exposes a public generic type `OrderedMap<V>` — an
insertion-ordered string-keyed map — with `new`, `len`, `is_empty`,
`contains_key`, `get`, `get_mut`, `insert`, `remove`, `iter` (yielding
`(&String, &V)` in insertion order), `keys`, and `values`, plus serde
`Serialize`/`Deserialize` that round-trips as a JSON object preserving key order.

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml` (depend
on the `schemas_ordered_map` crate by path — it lives at `../schemas_ordered_map`)
and use its `OrderedMap<V>` for every ordered-map field below. Do **not**
reimplement an ordered map here, and do not depend on any hand-written crate —
depend only on the generated sibling `schemas_ordered_map` member crate. This
sibling path dependency is part of the same workspace, not an external crates.io
dependency, so add it even though the general guidance is to avoid outside
dependencies.

## The types

Expose these public types, all deriving serde serialize + deserialize, equality,
and clone:

- `PromptTargetRecord`: a per-target record inside a prompt entry, with public
  fields:
  - `prompt_hash_at_gen: Option<String>`, JSON key **`promptHashAtGen`**, omitted
    from output when `None`, defaulting to `None` when absent.
  - `files: Vec<String>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
  - `unattributed: Option<bool>`, omitted from output when `None`, defaulting to
    `None` when absent.
- `PromptRecord`: a prompt entry, with public fields:
  - `module: String`.
  - `prompt_hash: String`, JSON key **`promptHash`**.
  - `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, JSON key **`declaredTargets`**, defaulting to
    empty when absent.
  - `targets: OrderedMap<PromptTargetRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
- `FileRecord`: a file entry, with public fields:
  - `target: String`.
  - `hash: String`.
  - `prompts: Vec<String>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
- `NaplMap`: the whole map, with public fields:
  - `version: u32`, defaulting to `2` when absent.
  - `prompts: OrderedMap<PromptRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.
  - `files: OrderedMap<FileRecord>`, defaulting to empty when absent.

And these plain data structs (comparable/cloneable; no serde needed):

- `ModuleFile` with public `target: String` and `file_path: String`.
- `FileInput` with public `file_path: String` and `hash: String`.
- `AttributionInput` with public `rel: String`, `module: String`,
  `prompt_hash: String`, `target: String`, `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, and
  `files: Vec<FileInput>`.
- `UnattributedInput` with public `rel: String`, `module: String`,
  `prompt_hash: String`, `target: String`, `declared_targets: Vec<String>`, and
  `files: Vec<String>`.

## Construction and (de)serialization

- `empty_map() -> NaplMap`: version 2, empty prompts, empty files.
- `parse_map(raw: &str) -> Result<NaplMap, _>`: deserialize a JSON string; then
  if `version` is not `2`, it is a validation error ("unsupported map version").
  Corrupt JSON is a deserialization error. Neither panics.
- `map_to_json(map: &NaplMap) -> String`: pretty-print the map as JSON and append
  a single trailing newline. A written map re-parses to an equal map.

## Query helpers

- `prompts_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<String>`: the relative prompt keys
  whose record's `module` equals `module`, in insertion order.
- `has_module(map, module) -> bool`: whether the module has any prompts.
- `declared_targets_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<String>`: the union of the
  `declared_targets` across the module's prompt records, deduplicated, in first-
  seen order.
- `files_for_module(map, module) -> Vec<ModuleFile>`: for each of the module's
  prompts, for each `(target, target_record)` pair in insertion order, each file
  path in `target_record.files`, deduplicated by the `(target, file_path)` pair,
  in first-seen order.
- `is_prompt_gen_stale(record: Option<&PromptRecord>, target, prompt_hash, force) -> bool`:
  `true` if `force`; else `true` if the record is absent; else `true` if the
  record has no target sub-record for `target`; else `true` if that sub-record's
  `unattributed` is `Some(true)`; else `true` if its `prompt_hash_at_gen` is
  absent or differs from `prompt_hash`; otherwise `false`.

## Mutation helpers

Both mutate the passed map in place.

### `record_attribution(map, input: &AttributionInput)`

Records a successful generation. Let `existing_targets` be the current prompt
record's `targets` (if the prompt `rel` already exists), and `prior_files` be the
files the `input.target` sub-record previously listed. For every prior file that
is **not** among the new `input.files` paths, detach it (see "Detaching" below).
Then set the `input.target` sub-record to a `PromptTargetRecord` with
`prompt_hash_at_gen = Some(input.prompt_hash)`, `files` = the new file paths in
order, and `unattributed = None`, keeping any other targets. Store the prompt
record under `input.rel` with `module`, `prompt_hash`, `declared_targets`, and
those `targets`. Finally, for each new file, ensure a `FileRecord` under its path
with `target = input.target`, `hash` = that file's hash, and a `prompts` list
that includes `input.rel` (appended once if not already present, preserving any
existing prompts for a file shared across prompts).

### `record_unattributed(map, input: &UnattributedInput)`

Records a generation that produced files but could not be attributed. As above,
detach **every** prior file of the `input.target` sub-record. Then set that
sub-record to `prompt_hash_at_gen = None`, `files = input.files`, and
`unattributed = Some(true)`, keeping other targets, and store the prompt record
under `input.rel`. Do **not** create `FileRecord` entries for the files.

### Detaching a file from a prompt

To detach `file_path` from prompt `rel`: if the map has a `FileRecord` for
`file_path`, remove `rel` from its `prompts`; if no prompts remain, remove the
file entry entirely; otherwise keep the file entry with the reduced `prompts`.

This is why re-attributing a prompt to a strictly smaller set of files drops the
now-orphaned file entry, and why marking a target unattributed detaches all of
its files. A subsequent successful `record_attribution` for the same prompt/target
overwrites the sub-record, clearing the `unattributed` flag and restoring a
`prompt_hash_at_gen`.

events.content:
[package]
name = "schemas_map"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
schemas_ordered_map = { path = "../schemas_ordered_map" }

[dev-dependencies]
serde_json = "1"

entries.note: import OrderedMap from schemas_ordered_map sibling crate
promptHashAtGen field, omit if None, default None
declared_targets field renamed declaredTargets, default empty
prompts and files OrderedMap fields default empty
parse_map deserializes JSON and validates version
prompts_for_module filters by module, insertion order
detach_file helper removes prompt from file record
compute existing_targets, prior_files, detach stale files
set target sub-record with prompt_hash_at_gen/files/unattributed None
ensure FileRecord per new file, append prompt if absent
detach every prior file of target sub-record
store prompt record, no FileRecord created

entry.message: Agent scoped work correctly to schemas_map/, root Cargo.toml untouched.
entry.reasoning: Prompt required leaving workspace root manifest alone and touching nothing outside schemas_map/. Verified only schemas_map/Cargo.toml and schemas_map/src/lib.rs were created; root manifest was not modified. No ambiguity, just confirming correct scope discipline.

entry.message: FileRecord fields overwritten unconditionally on every attribution, even for pre-existing files.
entry.reasoning: Spec says "ensure a FileRecord under its path with target=input.target, hash=that file's hash, and a prompts list that includes input.rel." It doesn't explicitly say whether target/hash on an already-existing FileRecord (e.g. shared across prompts under a different target) should be overwritten or left alone if it already matches a different owner. Agent chose to always overwrite target and hash on the existing record while only appending to prompts if absent, which is a reasonable reading of "ensure" but not the only one (an alternate reading only fills in missing records and never touches existing target/hash of a shared file record). Not tested by the shipped test suite for a cross-target conflict case.
entry.suggestion: Clarify whether target/hash should be overwritten when a FileRecord for the given path already exists under a different attribution.

entry.message: files_for_module dedup logic matches spec exactly (dedup by target+path, first-seen order), verified by test.
entry.reasoning: No ambiguity found; implementation and accompanying test (files_for_module_dedupes_by_target_and_path) directly mirror the prompt's requirement.
